 Artificial Intelligence In Customer Service
   • Sentiment Analysis of Social Media Data
   •   Unstructured data: Customer complaints, opinions, feelings




   • Product Reviews Analysis on Amazon
       • Unstructured data reflecting genuine customer experiences/ opinions
       • Extract key topics discussed in the reviews
       • Identify frequent complaints, issues and pain points


                                                                           8
 Artificial Intelligence In Customer Service
 •   Facial recognition/Image analysis in retail store
 •   Audio analysis for customer support call recordings




 •   Voice assistants and speech analysis
     • Call Center recordings, voicemail messages
     • Identify the emotional tone of the speech
     • Provide instant responses to customer queries, order tracking, FAQs

                                                                             9
 Why Feature Extraction Matters?
Transforming Raw Data
• Problem: Diverse data formats
   Images, videos, text, audio
• Solution: Turn unstructured data into structured data
   Suitable to be fed into ML algorithms

Reducing Dimensionality
• Problem                                                                We will use PCA in
   • High dimensionality of unstructured data (too many variables)       Hand-On Exercise 2
   • Noise and irrelevant information
• Solution
   • Reduce the number of variables by identifying the most relevant features
   • Example: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)

 • Sentiment Analysis

                                  Hand-On Exercise 1




Inference using a pre-trained model

                                                      13
 Inference using a pre-trained model in Python or R

 Objective
 Let’s use the pre-trained AI model we generated in week 1 (from Teachable
 Machine) in Python or R
 • Dataset
 • Sample images from the Fruits 360 dataset of 90,380 images of 131 fruits
   and vegetables are made available on Moodle for download (under Week 1
   section)
 • Model type: Convolutional Neural Network
                                              Please use your preferred language!
 • Environment
                                                                 or


                                                                                    14
 How to use Python / R in Google Colab?
• In Google Colab, you can create Python or R notebooks to run
  analytics
• Notebooks can be created from scratch or uploaded as .ipynb files
• Two notebook files are available on Moodle
 • Week4_Hands_On_Exercise1_Python.ipynb
 • Week4_Hands_On_Exercise1_R.ipynb
• They contain Python and R code respectively for this Hands-On
  exercise



                                                                  15
 Inference using a pre-trained model in Python or R




                                                     16
 Inference using a pre-trained model in Python or R

  # Load the libraries needed, including numpy, tensorflow    # Install the keras library
  and keras                                                   install.packages("keras")
  import numpy as np
  import tensorflow as tf                                     # Load the keras library
  from tensorflow import keras                                library(keras)
  from PIL import Image, ImageOps #Install pillow instead
  of PIL                                                      # Load your pre-trained model trained during week 1 along
                                                              with the labels
  # Load your pre-trained model trained during week 1 along   model = load_model_hdf5("/content/keras_model.h5")
  with the labels                                             labels = as.vector(read.delim("/content/labels.txt",
  model =                                                     header=FALSE)$V1)
  keras.models.load_model("/content/keras_model.h5",
  compile=False)                                              # Load one image to classify and resise it to be at least
  class_names = open('/content/labels.txt',                   224x224
  'r').readlines()                                            img_path = "/content/pomegrenate.jpg"
                                                              img <- image_load(img_path, target_size = c(224,224))
  # Load one image to classify and resise it to be at least
  224x224
  image =
  Image.open('/content/pomegrenate.jpg').convert('RGB')
  image = ImageOps.fit(image, (224, 224), Image.BICUBIC)




                                                                                                                          17
 Inference using a pre-trained model in Python or R

  # Pre-process the image including:                         # Pre-process the image including:
  # Turn the image into a numpy array                        # Turn the image into a numpy array
  image_array = np.asarray(image)                            img_tensor <- image_to_array(img)
                                                             # Normalize the image
  # Normalize the image                                      img_tensor <- (img_tensor / 127.0) - 1
  normalized_image_array = (image_array.astype(np.float32)   # Create the array of the right shape to feed into the
  / 127.0) - 1                                               keras model
                                                             img_tensor <- array_reshape(img_tensor, c(1, 224, 224,
  # Create the array of the right shape to feed into the     3))
  keras model
  data = np.ndarray(shape=(1, 224, 224, 3),                  # Make predictions then display the label with the
  dtype=np.float32)                                          highest prediction probability
  # Load the image into the array                            preds = model %>% predict(img_tensor)
  data[0] = normalized_image_array                           cat("Class: ", labels[[which.max(preds)]], "\n")
                                                             cat("Confidence score: ", preds[[which.max(preds)]],
  # Make predictions then display the label with the         "\n")
  highest prediction probability
  prediction = model.predict(data)
  index = np.argmax(prediction)
  class_name = class_names[index]
  confidence_score = prediction[0][index]

  print('Class:', class_name, end='')
  print('Confidence score:', confidence_score)

                                                20
 Deep Learning for
Unstructured Data Analysis

                             Deep Neural Networks




                                                    21
 Deep Learning Models for Unstructured Data
• Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
  What are CNNs?
 • Automatically and adaptively learn spatial hierarchies of features from input
   images
 • Applications
    • Image recognition
    • Video analysis
    • Medical imaging diagnostics



                                                                                   22
 Deep Learning Models for Unstructured Data
• Recurrent Neural Networks / Long Short-Term Memory Networks
   What are RNNs/LSTMs?
 • Suited for sequential data like text and speech
  • Applications
       • Natural Language Processing (NLP): Tasks such as language
         modelling, translation
       • Speech Recognition: Convert spoken language into text
       • Text Generation: Create new text based on input sequences



                                                                     23
 Deep Learning Models for Unstructured Data

• Transformers
  What are Transformers?
 • Process input data in parallel, which significantly accelerates and optimises
   the training process: Faster and more efficient training!
   Popular Models:
     • BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers)
     • GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer)
  • Applications
       • NLP Tasks: Language understanding, question answering, text
         generation

                                                                                   24
 Deep Learning Models for Unstructured Data
• Autoencoders
 • Neural networks designed to copy their inputs to their outputs
 Applications
  • Feature learning: Extract meaningful features from raw data
  • Anomaly detection: Identify unusual patterns or outliers
  •   Noise reduction: Clean up data by removing noise
 • Variational autoencoders (VAEs)
  •   Used for generating complex data like images and music



                                                                    25
  Deep Learning Models for Unstructured Data
• Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)
 • Consist of two networks: a generator and a discriminator
  • How they work?
  Generator: Creates new data samples
  Discriminator: Evaluates the authenticity of the generated samples
    • Applications
    • Enhancing and Generating Art: Create new artworks/ improve existing ones
    • Generating Realistic Images: Produce lifelike images from random noise
    • Video Generation and Editing: Generate and edit video content


                                                                               26
 Artificial Intelligence in Media and Entertainment

• Applications
 •   Content recommendation
 •   Custom experiences
• Type of ML models
 •   Deep learning
 •   Generative AI




                                                     27
 Artificial Intelligence in Media and Entertainment

    1   Identify the Business Problem

•   Content Generation: Producing articles, music, videos, and games at scale
•   Tailored Content Recommendations to match user preferences
•   Immersive Media: Creating engaging and interactive media experiences
•   Efficiency and Cost Reduction: Streamlining content creation
        • To save time and reduce production costs
•   Engagement and Retention: Boosting viewer or user engagement and
    retention rates with immersive content



                                                                                28
 Artificial Intelligence in Media and Entertainment

    2   Translate the Business Problem into a Machine Learning Problem

•   Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision: For content generation,
       • NLP to create textual content
       • Computer vision for images and videos
•   Recommendation Systems: Building ML models that learn users'
    preferences/behaviours to personalise content recommendations
•   Automated Content Creation: Generative models like GANs (Generative
    Adversarial Networks) to create realistic images, videos, and music



                                                                          29
 Artificial Intelligence in Media and Entertainment

    3   Prepare Relevant Data (Collect, Clean, Transform)

•   Data Collection: Gather diverse datasets from content libraries, user
    interactions, and feedback
•   Data Cleaning: Remove irrelevant, duplicate, or poor-quality data
        • Enhance ML model performance
    Data Transformation: Convert data into a suitable format for ML models
         e.g., tokenize text or extract features from images/videos




                                                                             30
 Artificial Intelligence in Media and Entertainment

    4   Analyse the Data (Training, Testing)

•   Model Training
    • Use prepared data to train generative AI models
    • Adjust parameters to enhance creativity and relevance of content
•   Model Testing and Evaluation
    • Test the models against a separate dataset
       • Evaluate performance, using metrics like accuracy, novelty, and user
         engagement



                                                                                31
 Artificial Intelligence in Media and Entertainment

    6   Generate Actionable Insights from the Key Findings

•   Enhanced Personalisation: Highly personalised media experiences
        • Watching movies, listening to music or podcasts, reading books
        • To drive engagement, satisfaction, and loyalty
•   Innovative Products and Services: Create new forms of entertainment and
    interactive experiences,
        • e.g. AI-generated music, VR environment, or personalised storytelling
•   Operational Efficiency: Automate content and production tasks
        • Creators can focus on strategic and creative work!

                                           Hand-On Deep Dive




Let’s build an image classifier in Python or R




                                                              39
 Let’s build an image classifier in Python or R

 Objective
 Train a model in Python or R to classify images into different
 categories
 • Dataset
   o Training.zip is a zip file containing 600 images (300 cheese and 300 pepperoni) to be used
     for training the model
   o Testing.zip is a zip file containing 200 images (100 cheese and 100 pepperoni) to be used
     for testing the model
 Each zip file contains two subfolders, one for images of plain cheese pizza, and one for images
 of pepperoni pizza).
 The images are a subset of an annotated multi-ingredient pizza image dataset called Pizza10
 https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.02821
